<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(observer);
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(target);

    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addObserver(observer, target);

    target->valueChanged(true);

    return std::make_pair(observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount());
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.registerThread();
    if (enableSafety) {
        thread_manager.enableThreadSafety(enableSafety);
    }
    return thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 * @return int The final value after atomic addition

int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    auto var = reaction::VarExpr<int>(initialValue);
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(var, addValue);
    return var.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *

 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return true if all batch operations succeed, false otherwise
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_unique<ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_unique<ObserverNode>();

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Create a batch ID
    void* batchId = graph.createBatch();

    // Register nodes to the batch
    graph.registerNodeToBatch(node1.get(), batchId);
    graph.registerNodeToBatch(node2.get(), batchId);

    // Verify nodes are in the batch
    auto nodesInBatch = graph.getNodesInBatch(batchId);
    if (nodesInBatch.size() != 2 ||
        std::find(nodesInBatch.begin(), nodesInBatch.end(), node1.get()) == nodesInBatch.end() ||
        std::find(nodesInBatch.begin(), nodesInBatch.end(), node2.get()) == nodesInBatch.end()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Unregister the batch
    graph.closeBatch(batchId);

    // Verify nodes are no longer in the batch
    nodesInBatch = graph.getNodesInBatch(batchId);
    if (!nodesInBatch.empty()) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of the reset method in TestObserverClosure
class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    void reset() {
        m_callCount = 0;
        m_changed = false;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

/**
 * Tests the closure of an observer node to ensure observer relationships are properly cleaned up.
 *
 * @param nodeName The name of the observer node to create and test.
 * @return true if the node closure worked correctly, false otherwise.
 */
bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addNode(observer);

    auto source = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>("source");
    graph.addNode(source);

    graph.addObserver(observer, source);

    // First notification: observer should be triggered
    source->notify();
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset the observer's state
    observer->reset();

    // Close the observer node
    graph.closeNode(observer);

    // Second notification: observer should not be triggered
    source->notify();
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 0) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Set up initial relationship
    graph.addObserver(source, target);

    // Step 4: Notify target and verify relationship works
    source->changed();
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 5: Reset observer for next test
    source->reset();
    target->reset();

    // Step 6: Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
    try {
        // Try to add an observer from target to source, creating a cycle
        graph.updateObserversTransactional(
            target,
            std::vector<std::shared_ptr<reaction::ObserverNode>>{source},
            std::vector<std::shared_ptr<reaction::ObserverNode>>{}
        );
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        // Cycle detected, proceed
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        // Other exceptions, return false
        return false;
    }

    // Step 8: Notify target again
    source->changed();

    // Step 9: Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 10: Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Initial setup: source observes target
    graph.addObserver(source, target);

    // Verify initial notification
    source->changed();
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset nodes
    source->reset();
    target->reset();

    // Attempt to create a cycle: target observes source
    try {
        graph.updateObserversTransactional(
            target,
            std::vector<std::shared_ptr<reaction::ObserverNode>>{source},
            std::vector<std::shared_ptr<reaction::ObserverNode>>{}
        );
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        // Cycle detected, expected
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        return false; // Unexpected exception
    }

    // Re-notify and verify original state
    source->changed();
    return !target->wasChanged() && target->getCallCount() == 1;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Create and register target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    graph.registerNode(target);

    // Create and register observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto observer = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        graph.registerNode(observer);
        observers.push_back(observer);
    }

    // Add observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }

    // Trigger notifications
    target->notify();

    // Verify notifications
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }

    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, allNotified);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);

    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->addObCb(target, observer1);
    graph->addObCb(observer1, observer2);

    target->notify();

    bool chainStatus = observer1->wasChanged() && observer2->wasChanged();
    int totalCalls = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, chainStatus);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test reactive expression using expr API
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @return int The result of (a + b) * 2 using expr syntax
 */
int test_reactive_expression(int a, int b) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr::create(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr::create(b);
    
    // Create reactive expression: (a + b) * 2
    auto expr = reaction::MulOpExpr::create(
        reaction::AddOpExpr::create(varA, varB),
        reaction::ConstExpr::create(2)
    );
    
    // Evaluate and return the result
    return expr->evaluate();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

namespace test_reaction {

}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    auto node = std::make_shared<reaction::Node>();
    reaction::VarExpr<int> varA(node, a);
    reaction::VarExpr<int> varB(node, b);
    reaction::CalcExpr<int> firstCalc(node, [varA = varA, varB = varB](const reaction::NodePtr& node) {
        return varA->get() + varB->get();
    });
    reaction::CalcExpr<int> secondCalc(node, [firstCalc = firstCalc](const reaction::NodePtr& node) {
        return firstCalc->get() * 2;
    });

    varA->set(newA);

    return secondCalc->get();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {
    reaction::ReactionGraph g;
    auto var_a = std::make_shared<reaction::Var<int>>(a);
    auto var_b = std::make_shared<reaction::Var<int>>(b);
    auto var_c = std::make_shared<reaction::Var<int>>(c);
    auto calc = std::make_shared<reaction::Calc<int>>();
    
    g.addNode(var_a);
    g.addNode(var_b);
    g.addNode(var_c);
    g.addNode(calc);
    
    // Initial calculation: a + b
    calc->setSource([=]() { return var_a->get() + var_b->get(); }, {var_a, var_b});
    
    // Reset calculation to: b * c
    calc->setSource([=]() { return var_b->get() * var_c->get(); }, {var_b, var_c});
    
    return calc->getValue();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    auto calc = reaction::Calc<std::string>::create(
        [this](const std::string& name, int age) {
            return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
        },
        person.m_name, person.m_age);

    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);

    return calc->get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    Var<int> a(initialA);
    Var<int> b(initialB);
    Var<int> recomputeCount(0);

    CalcExpr<int> sumExpr = a + b;
    sumExpr.addListener([&recomputeCount](const NodePtr& node, const ValuePtr& value) {
        recomputeCount.set(recomputeCount.get() + 1);
    });

    // Non-batch updates
    a.set(nonBatchA);
    b.set(nonBatchB);
    int nonBatchCount = recomputeCount.get();

    // Batch updates
    Batch batch([this](const NodePtr& node) {
        a.set(batchA);
        b.set(batchB);
    });
    batch.execute();
    int batchCount = recomputeCount.get();

    return std::make_tuple(sumExpr.get(), nonBatchCount, batchCount);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    reaction::Var<int> varA(valueA, "varA");
    reaction::Var<int> varB(valueB, "varB");
    
    // Create a calculation that depends on varA and varB, with a name
    reaction::Calc<int> calcAB([=]() {
        return varA.get() + varB.get();
    }, "calcAB");
    
    // Create an action with a name (no dependencies needed here)
    reaction::Action actionAB("actionAB");
    
    // Retrieve names and combine them into a string
    std::ostringstream oss;
    oss << varA.getName() << "," 
        << varB.getName() << "," 
        << calcAB.getName() << "," 
        << actionAB.getName();
    
    return oss.str();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    auto sensor = std::make_shared<Var<double>>(initialValue);
    auto triggerOp = std::make_shared<FilterTrigOp<double>>(sensor, [diffThreshold](double curr, double prev) {
        return std::abs(curr - prev) >= diffThreshold;
    });
    auto statusOp = std::make_shared<CalcOp<std::string>>(sensor, [levelThreshold](const NodePtr& node) {
        double val = node->getValue<double>();
        return val > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });
    auto countVar = std::make_shared<Var<int>>(0);
    auto actionOp = std::make_shared<ActionOp>([countVar](const NodePtr& node) {
        int current = countVar->getValue();
        countVar->updateValue(current + 1);
    });

    // Connect reactive nodes
    sensor->addObCb(triggerOp);
    triggerOp->addObCb(statusOp);
    triggerOp->addObCb(actionOp);

    // Update sensor with first value and record count
    sensor->updateValue(firstUpdate);
    int count1 = countVar->getValue();

    // Update sensor with second value and record count
    sensor->updateValue(secondUpdate);
    int count2 = countVar->getValue();

    // Get final status
    std::string status = statusOp->getValue();

    return std::make_tuple(status, count1, count2);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged
inline void FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged([[maybe_unused]] bool changed = true) {
    ++m_notificationCount;
}

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount
inline int FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount() const {
    return m_notificationCount;
}

// Implementation of ProfileFields constructor
inline ProfileFields::ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
    : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}

// Implementation of ProfileFields::score and active accessors
inline reaction::Var<int>& ProfileFields::score() { return m_score; }
inline reaction::Var<bool>& ProfileFields::active() { return m_active; }

// Implementation of test_field_binding
inline FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    auto fields = std::make_shared<ProfileFields>(initialScore, true);
    auto node = std::make_shared<FieldOwnerNode>();
    
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(node);
    
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.bindFields(fields, node);
    
    // Update score and record notifications
    fields->score() = initialScore + scoreDelta;
    int notificationsAfterScore = node->getNotificationCount();
    
    // Update active and record notifications
    fields->active() = !fields->active().get();
    int notificationsAfterStatus = node->getNotificationCount();
    
    return FieldBindingResult{
        .notificationsAfterScoreUpdate = notificationsAfterScore,
        .notificationsAfterStatusUpdate = notificationsAfterStatus
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::createVar(baseValue);
    // Create reactive expression (depends on var)
    auto expr = reaction::createExpr([=]() { return var; }, var);
    // Create reactive calculation (square of var)
    auto calc = reaction::createCalc([=]() { return var * var; }, var);
    
    // Step 4: Get initial expression value
    int initialExpr = expr.get();
    // Step 5: Update reactive variable
    var.set(baseValue + increment);
    // Step 6: Get updated expression value
    int updatedExpr = expr.get();
    // Step 7: Get squared value after update
    int squared = calc.get();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        .initialExpressionValue = initialExpr,
        .updatedExpressionValue = updatedExpr,
        .squaredValueAfterUpdate = squared
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

NamedObserverNode::NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
const std::string& NamedObserverNode::name() const { return m_name; }

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    auto a = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("A");
    auto b = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("B");
    auto c = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("C");

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(a);
    graph.registerNode(b);
    graph.registerNode(c);

    graph.addObserver(a, b);
    graph.addObserver(b, c);

    reaction::NodeSet observersSet;
    graph.collectObservers(a, observersSet);

    bool hasDirect = false;
    bool hasTransitive = false;
    for (const auto& wp : observersSet) {
        if (auto ptr = wp.lock()) {
            if (ptr->name() == "B") hasDirect = true;
            if (ptr->name() == "C") hasTransitive = true;
        }
    }

    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observersSet.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = hasDirect,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = hasTransitive
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    // Register the main thread
    threadManager.registerThread();

    auto before = ThreadSafetyStatus{
        .safetyBefore = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled(),
        .threadCountBefore = threadManager.getThreadCount(),
    };

    // Create and start worker thread
    std::thread worker([]() {
        auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
        tm.registerThread();
    });
    worker.join();

    auto after = ThreadSafetyStatus{
        .safetyAfter = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled(),
        .threadCountAfter = threadManager.getThreadCount(),
    };

    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        .safetyBefore = before.safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfter = after.safetyAfter,
        .threadCountBefore = before.threadCountBefore,
        .threadCountAfter = after.threadCountAfter
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    BatchLifecycleResult result;

    // 1. Create reactive variable
    reaction::Var<int> var(initialValue);

    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;

    // 3. Create calculation that triggers recompute count
    auto calc = reaction::Calc<int>([this](int v) {
        recomputeCount++;
        return v;
    }, var);

    // 4. Create Batch object
    reaction::Batch batch;

    // 5. Check if batch is closed initially
    result.closedInitially = batch.isClosed();

    // 6. Execute batch
    batch.add([&var](const NodePtr& node) {
        var.set(updatedValue);
    });
    batch.execute();

    // 7. Record recompute count after execute
    result.recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount;

    // 8. Close batch
    batch.close();

    // 9. Check if batch is closed after close
    result.closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();

    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

    graph.cleanupOldEntries();

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>
#include <mutex>
#include <shared_mutex>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Step 2: Reset thread manager state
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // Step 3: Register current thread
    thread_manager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    // Step 4: Get thread count after registration
    auto thread_count = thread_manager.getThreadCount();
    
    // Step 5: Get thread safety status before enabling
    auto before_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Step 6: Manually enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Step 7: Get thread safety status after enabling
    auto after_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Step 8: Reset thread manager state again
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // Step 9: Get thread safety status after reset
    auto after_reset = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Step 10: Return the transition results
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = before_enable,
        .afterEnable = after_enable,
        .afterReset = after_reset,
        .threadCount = thread_count
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

template <typename Op, typename L, typename R>
using BinaryOpExpr = CalcOpExpr<CalcOp<Op, L, R>>;

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    auto graph = std::make_shared<Graph>();
    auto mutableVar = graph->createVar(baseValue);
    auto constVar = graph->createConst(constValue);
    auto addOp = std::make_shared<AddOp<mutableVar::value_type, constVar::value_type>>();
    addOp->setLeft(mutableVar);
    addOp->setRight(constVar);
    auto sumOp = graph->createCalc(addOp);

    int initialSum = sumOp->getValue();
    mutableVar->update(baseValue + delta);
    int updatedSum = sumOp->getValue();
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;
};

template <typename T>
using NodePtr = std::shared_ptr<T>;

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    auto varExpr = NodePtr<VarExpr<int>>(std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(initialValue));
    auto counterVar = NodePtr<VarExpr<int>>(std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(0));

    auto calcExpr = NodePtr<CalcExpr<int>>(std::make_shared<CalcExpr<int>>());
    calcExpr->setOpExpr(MulOpExpr{varExpr, 2});
    calcExpr->addObCb(varExpr);
    calcExpr->setTrigMode(AlwaysTrig{});
    calcExpr->addAction([counterVar.get()]() {
        counterVar->add(1);
    });

    int recomputeCountAfterInit = counterVar->getValue();
    varExpr->update(initialValue);
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = counterVar->getValue();
    int dummy = calcExpr->getValue(); // Force evaluation

    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        recomputeCountAfterInit,
        recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    bool safetyBeforeGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    std::thread worker([this]() {
        {
            reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard g("TestWorker");
        }
    });

    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfterGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    return ThreadGuardState{
        .safetyBeforeGuard = safetyBeforeGuard,
        .safetyAfterGuard = safetyAfterGuard,
        .threadCountBeforeGuard = threadCountBeforeGuard,
        .threadCountAfterGuard = threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Step 1: Create a reactive variable
    auto var = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Create a calculation with LastHandle policy
    auto calc = createCalc([var = var](const NodePtr& node) {
        return var->get();
    }, LastHandle{});
    
    // Step 3: Get initial result
    int initialResult = calc->get();
    
    // Step 4: Update the source variable
    var->set(updatedValue);
    
    // Step 5: Get updated result
    int updatedResult = calc->get();
    
    // Step 6: Close the source node
    var->close();
    
    // Step 7: Get result after source close
    int closedResult = calc->get();
    
    // Step 8: Return snapshot
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = closedResult
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>
```cpp
#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StorageInfo {
    bool is_sbo;
    std::size_t stack_usage;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    auto make_large = [this](int seed) {
        LargePayload p;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            p.data[i] = seed + i;
        }
        return p;
    };

    SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallRes(initialSmall);
    SBOResource<LargePayload> largeRes(make_large(largeSeed));

    bool changed = smallRes.update(updatedSmall);

    auto smallInfo = smallRes.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeRes.getStorageInfo();

    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = changed,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.is_sbo,
        .smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stack_usage,
        .largeInitialized = true,
        .largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.is_sbo
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_shared_mutex.h>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    SharedMutexResult result{false, false, false, false};
    
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;
    
    // Step 4-5: Try shared lock without safety
    result.trySharedWithoutSafety = mutex.trySharedNoSafety();
    if (result.trySharedWithoutSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }
    
    {
        // Step 6-8: Use lock guard and try unique lock without safety
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lock_guard(mutex);
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.tryUniqueNoSafety();
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }
    
    // Step 10: Enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Step 11-12: Try shared lock with safety
    result.trySharedWithSafety = mutex.tryShared();
    if (result.trySharedWithSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }
    
    {
        // Step 13-15: Use lock guard and try unique lock with safety
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lock_guard(mutex);
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = mutex.tryUnique();
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }
    
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    SboTraitSummary summary;

    TinyPod tiny;

    summary.tinyForceHeap = tinyTraits.m_force_heap;

    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = mediumTraits.m_conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = mediumTraits.m_force_heap;

    // Analyze LargePod SBO traits
    LargePod large;
    const auto& largeTraits = reaction::SBOTraits::getInstance().getTypeTraits(large);
    summary.largeForceHeap = largeTraits.m_force_heap;

    // Get SBO configuration thresholds
    const auto& config = reaction::SBOTraits::getInstance().getConfig();
    summary.alwaysThreshold = config.m_sbo_always_threshold;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = config.m_sbo_conditional_threshold;
    summary.maxStackUsage = config.m_sbo_max_stack_usage;

    return summary;
}

} // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;
};

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    auto &threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    auto varExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::VarExpr>(initialValue);
    auto calcExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcExpr>(
        [multiplier = multiplier](const reaction::NodePtr &node) {
            return node->get<int>() * multiplier;
        },
        varExpr
    );

    int beforeResetValue = calcExpr->get<int>();
    threadManager.enableSafety();
    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isSafetyEnabled();
    calcExpr->reset();
    varExpr->set(initialValue + 1);
    int afterResetValue = calcExpr->get<int>();
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        .beforeResetValue = beforeResetValue,
        .afterResetValue = afterResetValue,
        .safetyEnabledDuringReset = safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace reaction {
class ReactionException : public std::runtime_error {
public:
    enum ErrorCode {
        UNKNOWN = 0,
        DEPENDENCY_CYCLE,
        SELF_OBSERVATION,
        NULL_POINTER_ACCESS,
        RESOURCE_NOT_INITIALIZED,
        TYPE_MISMATCH,
        INVALID_STATE,
        THREAD_SAFETY_VIOLATION,
        BATCH_OPERATION_CONFLICT,
        MEMORY_ALLOCATION_FAILURE,
        GRAPH_CORRUPTION,
        OBSERVER_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
    };

    // Assume this method exists
    static std::string errorCodeToString(ErrorCode code) {
        switch (code) {
        case ErrorCode::DEPENDENCY_CYCLE:
            return "DEPENDENCY_CYCLE";
        case ErrorCode::SELF_OBSERVATION:
            return "SELF_OBSERVATION";
        // ... other cases ...
        default:
            return "UNKNOWN";
        }
    }
};
} // namespace reaction

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>

namespace test_reaction {

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,

    info.line = ex.getLine();

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourcePointerSnapshot {
    int initialPointerValue;
    int pointerValueAfterWrite;
    bool pointerStable;
};

ResourcePointerSnapshot test_resource_pointer_view(int initialValue, int directWriteValue) {
    reaction::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    auto ptr1 = resource.getPtr().get();
    int initial = *ptr1;
    *ptr1 = directWriteValue;
    auto ptr2 = resource.getPtr().get();
    int after = *ptr2;
    bool stable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initial,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = after,
        .pointerStable = stable
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <shared_mutex.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct Counter {
    int value;
    void add(int delta) noexcept { value += delta; }
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate pointer-style access for reactive structs.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the Counter
 * @param delta Amount added on each pointer invocation
 * @return PointerAccessSnapshot Snapshot capturing results after pointer operations
 */
inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    auto counterPtr = std::make_shared<Counter>(Counter{initialValue});
    VarExpr<int> reactiveVar([counterPtr = counterPtr](const NodePtr& node) noexcept {
        return counterPtr->value;
    });

    // Step 3: Get first pointer
    Counter* ptr1 = counterPtr.get();
    // Step 4: Call add through pointer
    ptr1->add(delta);
    // Step 5: Get value after first add
    int valueAfterFirstAdd = reactiveVar.get();

    // Step 6: Get second pointer
    Counter* ptr2 = counterPtr.get();
    // Step 7: Call add through pointer again
    ptr2->add(delta);
    // Step 8: Get value after second add
    int valueAfterSecondAdd = reactiveVar.get();

    // Step 9: Check if pointer is stable
    bool pointerStable = ptr1 == ptr2;

    // Step 10: Return snapshot
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        .valueAfterFirstAdd = valueAfterFirstAdd,
        .valueAfterSecondAdd = valueAfterSecondAdd,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variable
    ReactVar<int> source(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Create copy of source
    auto copy = source.getPtr()->copy();
    
    // Step 3 & 4: Check validity and equality before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = !source.getPtr().expired();
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // Step 5: Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // Step 6: Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = !source.getPtr().expired();
    
    // Step 7: Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    // Step 8: Return validity snapshot
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        .firstValidBeforeClose = firstValidBeforeClose,
        .firstValidAfterClose = firstValidAfterClose,
        .copiesEqualBeforeClose = copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        .copiesEqualAfterClose = copiesEqualAfterClose,
        .equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables with given names
    auto var1 = reaction::createVar(0).setName(firstName);
    auto var2 = reaction::createVar(0).setName(secondName);
    auto var3 = reaction::createVar(0).setName(thirdName);

    // Insert into a set to order them
    std::set<reaction::React<int>> reactSet = {var1, var2, var3};

    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto &react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }

    // Check if all input names are unique
    bool allUnique = (firstName != secondName) &&
                     (secondName != thirdName) &&
                     (firstName != thirdName);

    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class ResetAwareNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(std::move(name)), m_notificationCount(0) {}
    int notifications() const noexcept { return m_notificationCount.load(); }
    void clear() noexcept { m_notificationCount.store(0); }
    void reset() noexcept { m_notificationCount.store(0); } // Assuming reset clears notification count
private:
    std::atomic<int> m_notificationCount;
};

struct ResetNodeResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeReset;
    bool notifiedAfterReset;
};

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName, const std::string &targetName) {
    auto source = std::make_unique<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_unique<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    graph->registerNode(source);
    graph->registerNode(target);
    
    source->addObCb(target);
    
    source->notify();
    bool notifiedBefore = (target->notifications() == 1);
    
    target->clear();
    
    target->reset();
    
    source->notify();
    bool notifiedAfter = (target->notifications() == 1);
    
    source->close();
    target->close();
    
    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

TrackingFieldNode::TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(std::move(name)) {}

inline void TrackingFieldNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
    m_notifications++;
}

inline int TrackingFieldNode::notifications() const {
    return m_notifications;
}

inline void TrackingFieldNode::clear() {
    m_notifications = 0;
}

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("Owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("Field");

    ObserverGraph &observerGraph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.addNode(owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(field);

    FieldGraph &fieldGraph = FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.registerObj(objectId, field);

    observerGraph.addObserver(field, owner);

    field->notify();
    bool notifiedBefore = (owner->notifications() == 1);

    owner->clear();

    observerGraph.closeObservers(field);

    fieldGraph.deleteObj(objectId);

    observerGraph.addObserver(field, owner);

    field->notify();
    bool notifiedAfter = (owner->notifications() == 1);

    owner->close();
    field->close();

    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>
#include <memory>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace test_reaction {

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    auto& sm = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance();
    
    // 1. Enable monitoring
    sm.enable();
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    sm.init();
    
    // 3. Get usage stats before guard
    auto statsBefore = sm.getUsageStats();
    
    // 4. Check template allocation allowed
    bool templateAllowed = sm.isTemplateAllocationAllowed();
    
    // 5. Check runtime allocation allowed
    bool runtimeAllowed = sm.isRuntimeAllocationAllowed();
    
    // 6. Create large StackGuard instance
    bool guardHeld = false;
    bool allocationBlocked = false;
    try {
        // Use a large size to test stack limits
        constexpr size_t LARGE_SIZE = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
        reaction::memory::StackGuard g(LARGE_SIZE);
        guardHeld = true;
        
        // 8. Check if allocation blocked while guard is held
        try {
            reaction::memory::StackGuard g2(1024); // Small size to test blocking
            allocationBlocked = false;
        } catch (...) {
            allocationBlocked = true;
        }
    } catch (...) {
        // Guard creation failed, treat as no guard held
        guardHeld = false;
    }
    
    // 9. Get usage stats after guard
    auto statsAfter = sm.getUsageStats();
    
    // 10. Return snapshot
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        .statsBeforeGuard = statsBefore,
        .statsAfterGuard = statsAfter,
        .templateAllocationAllowed = templateAllowed,
        .runtimeAllocationAllowed = runtimeAllowed,
        .guardHeld = guardHeld,
        .allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlocked
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto co = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto s1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto s2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto s3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();

    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->registerNode(s1);
    graph->registerNode(s2);
    graph->registerNode(s3);
    graph->registerNode(co);

    s1->addObserver(co);

    s1->notify();

    int first = co->notifications();

    co->removeObserver(s1);
    s2->addObserver(co);
    s3->addObserver(co);

    s1->notify();
    s2->notify();
    s3->notify();

    ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot snapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = first,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = co->notifications(),
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = co->notifications() - 1,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = co->notifications() - 2
    };

    graph->closeAll();
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    auto node = std::make_shared<reaction::Node>();
    reaction::Var<int> var{node, 5};
    reaction::Var<int> counter{node, 0};
    
    reaction::Calc<int> calc{node, reaction::varExpr(var), [this](int newValue) {
        counter() += 1;
    }};
    
    int afterAdd = var.atomicAddAssign(3);
    int afterMult = var.atomicMultiplyAssign(2);
    int afterAnd = var.atomicBitwiseAndAssign(15);
    int afterShift = var.atomicLeftShiftAssign(2);
    int postInc = var.atomicIncrementPost();
    int final = var();
    int evals = counter();
    
    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        .afterAddition = afterAdd,
        .afterMultiplication = afterMult,
        .afterBitwiseAnd = afterAnd,
        .afterShift = afterShift,
        .postIncrementReturn = postInc,
        .finalValue = final,
        .calcEvaluations = evals
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthOrderingSnapshot {
    bool initialComparatorResult;
    bool shallowBeforeDeep;
    bool deepBeforeShallow;
    bool nullComesFirst;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate ObserverNode::updateDepth cooperating with BatchCompare ordering.
 *
 * @return DepthOrderingSnapshot Comparator outcomes after adjusting node depths.
 */
inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    struct DepthProbeNode : ObserverNode {
        uint16_t depth;
        DepthProbeNode() : depth(0) {}
        uint16_t getDepth() const noexcept { return depth; }
        void setDepth(uint16_t d) noexcept { depth = d; }
    };

    auto& tm = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.enableThreadSafety();

    auto shallow = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    auto deep = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();

    NodeWeak shallow_weak(shallow);
    NodeWeak deep_weak(deep);

    auto batch_compare = BatchCompare([this](const NodePtr& a, const NodePtr& b) {
        if (!a && !b) return false;
        if (!a) return true;
        if (!b) return false;
        return a->getDepth() < b->getDepth();
    });

    bool initialResult = batch_compare.compare(shallow_weak, deep_weak);

    shallow->setDepth(1);
    deep->setDepth(2);

    bool shallowBeforeDeepResult = batch_compare.compare(shallow_weak, deep_weak);

    shallow->setDepth(2);

    bool deepBeforeShallowResult = batch_compare.compare(deep_weak, shallow_weak);

    NodeWeak null_weak(nullptr);
    bool nullComesFirstResult = batch_compare.compare(null_weak, shallow_weak);

    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        .initialComparatorResult = initialResult,
        .shallowBeforeDeep = shallowBeforeDeepResult,
        .deepBeforeShallow = deepBeforeShallowResult,
        .nullComesFirst = nullComesFirstResult
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variables
    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr<int>::create(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr<int>::create(b);

    // Step 2: Build negated sum expression
    auto sumExpr = reaction::make_binary_op(reaction::AddOp{}, varA, varB);
    auto negSumExpr = reaction::make_unary_op(reaction::NegOp{}, sumExpr);

    // Step 3: Create reactive boolean variable
    auto boolVar = reaction::VarExpr<bool>::create(false);

    // Step 4: Build logical NOT expression
    auto notBoolExpr = reaction::make_unary_op(reaction::NotOp{}, boolVar);

    // Step 5: Create reactive integer variable
    auto intVar = reaction::VarExpr<int>::create(5);

    // Step 6: Build bitwise NOT expression
    auto bitNotExpr = reaction::make_unary_op(reaction::BitwiseNotOp{}, intVar);

    // Step 7: Get initial values
    int initialNegatedSum = negSumExpr.get();
    bool initialLogicalNot = notBoolExpr.get();

    // Step 8: Update variables to trigger recomputation
    varA.set(a + 1);
    varB.set(b + 1);
    boolVar.set(true);
    intVar.set(3); // Example update to trigger change

    // Step 9: Get updated values
    int updatedNegatedSum = negSumExpr.get();
    bool updatedLogicalNot = notBoolExpr.get();
    int bitwiseNotValue = bitNotExpr.get();

    // Step 10: Return snapshot
    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .logicalNotAfterFlip = updatedLogicalNot,

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    reaction::VarExpr<bool> aVar(a), bVar(b), cVar(c);

    // Build logical expressions
    auto andExpr = aVar && bVar;
    auto orExpr = aVar || bVar;

    // Get initial results
    bool initialAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool initialOr = orExpr.get();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    aVar.set(!a);
    bVar.set(!b);

    // Get updated results
    bool updatedAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool updatedOr = orExpr.get();

    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/graph_cache.h>
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <memory>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot test_node_metrics_cache() {
    auto cache = NodeMetricsCache::getInstance();
    auto node = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    // Step 3: Get cache stats before caching
    const auto statsBefore = cache.getStats();

    // Step 4: Query metrics before caching (expect miss)
    auto metricsBefore = node->getMetrics();

    // Step 5: Cache node metrics explicitly
    cache.cacheNodeMetrics(node);

    // Step 6: Query metrics after caching (expect hit)
    auto metricsAfter = node->getMetrics();

    // Step 7: Get cache stats after caching
    const auto statsAfter = cache.getStats();

    // Step 8: Invalidate all cache entries
    cache.invalidateAll();

    // Step 9: Query metrics after invalidation (expect miss and re-cache)
    auto metricsInvalidated = node->getMetrics();

    // Step 10: Get cache stats after invalidation
    const auto statsInvalidated = cache.getStats();

    // Calculate hit ratio
    double hitRatio = 0.0;
    if (statsAfter.hitCount + statsAfter.missCount > 0) {
        hitRatio = static_cast<double>(statsAfter.hitCount) / (statsAfter.hitCount + statsAfter.missCount);
    }

    return NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = statsBefore.entries,
        .entriesAfterCache = statsAfter.entries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = statsInvalidated.entries,
        .hitCount = statsAfter.hitCount,
        .missCount = statsAfter.missCount,
        .hitRatio = hitRatio,
        .metricsCachedExists = (statsAfter.entries > 0),
        .metricsClearedAfterInvalidate = (statsInvalidated.entries == 0)
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

// Implementation of the test function
GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    // Step 1: Define IntCache class inheriting from CacheBase
    class IntCache : public CacheBase<int, int> {
    public:
        using CacheBase<int, int>::CacheBase; // Inherit constructors
        using CacheBase<int, int>::insert;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::get;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::getStats;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::handleInvalidation;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::triggerCleanup;
    };

    // Step 3: Create IntCache instance
    IntCache cache;

    // Step 4: Get initial cache statistics
    const auto initialStats = cache.getStats();
    bool firstMiss = true;
    bool secondHit = false;

    // Step 5: Perform initial lookup (expect miss)
    if (auto val = cache.get(42)) [[unlikely]] {
        firstMiss = false;
    }

    // Step 6: Insert value into cache
    cache.insert(42, 100);

    // Step 7: Perform second lookup (expect hit)
    if (auto val = cache.get(42)) [[likely]] {
        secondHit = true;
    }

    // Step 8: Get stats after insertion
    const auto statsAfterInsert = cache.getStats();

    // Step 9: Invalidate all cache entries
    cache.handleInvalidation(InvalidationStrategy::All);

    // Step 10: Trigger cleanup
    cache.triggerCleanup();

    // Step 11: Get stats after invalidation
    const auto statsAfterInvalidation = cache.getStats();

    // Step 12: Calculate hit ratio and assemble snapshot
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = initialStats.entries,
        .entriesAfterInsert = statsAfterInsert.entries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalidation.entries,
        .hitCount = statsAfterInsert.hitCount,
        .missCount = statsAfterInsert.missCount,
        .hitRatio = statsAfterInsert.hitCount + statsAfterInsert.missCount > 0 
                        ? static_cast<double>(statsAfterInsert.hitCount) 
                          / (statsAfterInsert.hitCount + statsAfterInsert.missCount) 
                        : 0.0,
        .firstLookupWasMiss = firstMiss,
        .secondLookupWasHit = secondHit
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/global_state.h>
#include <functional>

namespace test_reaction {

GlobalStateSnapshot test_global_state_scoped_guards() {
    // 1. Reset global state
    reaction::resetGlobalState();
    
    // 2. Create GlobalStateSnapshot struct
    GlobalStateSnapshot snapshot;
    snapshot.trackingActiveBefore = reaction::isTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveBefore = reaction::isBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveBefore = reaction::isBatchFunActive();
    
    // 3. Check state before guards
    
    // 4. Create RegFunGuard in scope block
    {
        reaction::RegFunGuard g(std::function<void(const NodePtr&)>([this](const NodePtr& node) {}));
        snapshot.trackingActiveInRegGuard = reaction::isTrackingActive();
    }
    
    // 5. Check state inside RegFunGuard scope
    
    // 6. Create BatchFunGuard in scope block
    {
        reaction::BatchFunGuard g(std::function<void(const NodePtr&)>([this](const NodePtr& node) {}));
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInBatchGuard = reaction::isBatchFunActive();
    }
    
    // 7. Check state inside BatchFunGuard scope
    
    // 8. Create BatchExeGuard in scope block
    {
        reaction::BatchExeGuard g();
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchExeGuard = reaction::isBatchActive();
    }
    
    // 9. Check state inside BatchExeGuard scope
    
    // 10. Reset global state again after guards
    reaction::resetGlobalState();
    
    // 11. Check state after guards
    snapshot.trackingActiveAfter = reaction::isTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveAfter = reaction::isBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveAfter = reaction::isBatchFunActive();
    
    // 12. Return snapshot struct
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <functional>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    ForceRegisterSnapshot snapshot;

    // Reset the thread manager state
    tm.reset();

    // Check thread safety before any registration
    snapshot.safetyBeforeForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // Register main thread
    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();

    // Check thread safety after main thread registration
    snapshot.safetyAfterMainForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // Create worker thread to register itself
    std::thread worker([&tm]() {
        tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    });

    // Wait for worker thread to complete
    worker.join();

    // Check thread safety after worker thread registration
    snapshot.safetyAfterWorkerForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;
    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    VarExpr<int> varA(a);
    VarExpr<int> varB(b);
    VarExpr<int> varC(c);

    // Build sum expression: A + B
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_expr(varA, varB, OpAdd);

    // Build negated expression: -(A + B)
    auto negatedExpr = make_unary_expr(sumExpr, OpNeg);

    // Build chained expression: -(A + B) * C
    auto chainedExpr = make_binary_expr(negatedExpr, varC, OpMul);

    // Capture initial values
    RawExpressionSnapshot snapshot{
        .initialSum = sumExpr(),
        .initialNegatedSum = negatedExpr(),
        .initialChainedValue = chainedExpr()
    };

    // Update variables and trigger recomputation
    varA.assign(a + 1);
    varC.assign(c + 1);
    varA.notify();
    varC.notify();

    // Capture updated values
    snapshot.updatedSum = sumExpr();
    snapshot.updatedNegatedSum = negatedExpr();
    snapshot.updatedChainedValue = chainedExpr();

    return snapshot;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

But the problem says to apply each operation in order. So the steps are:

1. Add assignment (+=)
2. Multiply assignment (*=)
3. Bitwise AND assignment (&=)
4. Left shift assignment (<<=)
5. Increment (++)

Each time, the helper function is called, and the change flag is recorded.

Now, putting all the code together.

The complete file would have the namespace, the IntAtomicImpl class with its methods, and the test function.

So the final code is as follows.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Implementation of the atomic helper wrapper for testing compound assignment operations.
 * Tracks value changes and reports whether each operation caused a change.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    template <typename F, typename T = ReturnType<F>>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = oldValue != m_value;
    }

    template <typename F, typename T = ReturnType<F>>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op, bool forceNotify) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = forceNotify || (oldValue != m_value);
    }

    int32_t value() const noexcept { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const noexcept { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    std::atomic<int32_t> m_value;
    std::atomic<bool> m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Executes a series of atomic compound assignment operations and records their effects.
 *
 * @param initial Starting value for the atomic operation.
 * @return A snapshot of the final state and change detection results.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl atomic(initial);

    bool addChanged = reaction::atomicAddAssign(atomic, 10);
    bool multiplyChanged = reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(atomic, 2);
    bool andChanged = reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(atomic, 0xF);
    bool leftShiftChanged = reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(atomic, 3);
    bool incrementChanged = reaction::atomicIncrement(atomic);

    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        .finalValue = atomic.value(),
        .addReportedChange = addChanged,
        .multiplyReportedChange = multiplyChanged,
        .andReportedChange = andChanged,
        .leftShiftReportedChange = leftShiftChanged,
        .incrementReportedChange = incrementChanged
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
